expectations.text: Session brief contains phase markers for all 12 phases
expectations.evidence: session-brief.md Phase Completion Markers section shows [P1✓] through [P11✓] explicitly, with Phase 12 documented in Phase Details section as 'Phase 12: Output Synthesis — All files written to docs/research/jsonplaceholder-typicode-com/'. All 12 phases have explicit markers or completion documentation.

expectations.text: Phase 12 runs only after P1-P11 markers are present
expectations.evidence: session-brief.md shows Phase Completion Markers section with all [P1✓]-[P11✓] listed sequentially before the Phase Details section which documents Phase 12 output. The ordering is structurally correct — P11 is marked complete before P12 documentation appears.
outputs/docs/research/jsonplaceholder-typicode-com/INDEX.md exists and is 1362 bytes — substantive content, not empty.

claims.evidence: session-brief.md states this in Phase 9 OSINT but cannot be verified from the static output files.
session-brief.md logs '[TECH-PACK-UNAVAILABLE:express:unknown]' in Phase 4 and in the Graceful Degradation Log — consistent with P4 being completed (not skipped) with a graceful degradation signal.

suggestions.assertion: Phase 12 runs only after P1-P11 markers are present
suggestions.reason: This assertion can only be verified from the session brief ordering, which is authored by the executor itself. A skill that writes P1-P11 markers at the end then runs P12 would still pass. Consider adding a timestamp or step-counter assertion to enforce actual ordering.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are solid for detecting the phase-skipping regression. The completion gate check is meaningful but relies on session-brief ordering, which could theoretically be gamed.
